{{Infobox concept
| name = Othaghor
| introduced = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
}}

''See also: [[Othaghor Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Othaghor is a [[Great Elder]] known as the [[Hordefather]], characterized by a profound vanity and pride. Unlike some of his peers who pursue cosmic goals like knowledge or annihilation, Othaghor's primary drive is the preservation of [[life]]. He possesses an unparalleled understanding of biological mechanics, allowing him to create a vast array of living creatures. These creations range from bizarre amphibian monsters and many-legged beasts to mindless, animalistic spawn that behave more like predators than entities with complex logic. His influence is felt through his amphibious hordes and various specialized creatures, such as the [[Bellowing Horror]]. Othaghor is also associated with a desert temple and has been historically buried by certain individuals. While he is viewed as a terrifying threat to humanity, his followers and representatives engage in diplomacy and trade, sometimes acting as business partners rather than mere masters of slaves.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Originally, the [[Greenwardens]] were established to counteract the worship of Othaghor. This entity is known for its ability to share bodies and contains a desert temple within itself. During the events of the story, [[Calder]] successfully conquered Othaghor's brood along with various unnamed monsters. Eventually, the [[Estyr Six]] managed to reach Othaghor, an entity driven by a desire to preserve [[life]] above all else.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]] is a figure whose worship the [[Greenwardens]] were originally meant to counteract.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c4">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]] is said to share bodies.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] conquered the unnamed monsters of [[Othaghor]] and his brood.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]] was reached by the [[Estyr Six]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]] wants to preserve [[life]] above all else.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Othaghor]] contains a desert temple.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28" />}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Othaghor]] is capable of taking bodies.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c6">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Othaghor]] is a possible deity or symbol worshipped by the [[Sleepless]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A voice like [[Othaghor]] bellowed behind [[Meia]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Bellowing Horror]] is a spawn of [[Othaghor]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Othaghor]] is dividing people up piece by piece to be devoured.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c18">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Othaghor]] is a [[Great Elder]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c1">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Othaghor]] is a figure associated with the era of the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Othaghor]] is a frog-faced [[Great Elder]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c18">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Othaghor]] has bizarre amphibian creatures.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c18" />}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Othaghor is an unknown entity characterized by endless biology involving constant mutation, consumption, and breeding. This creature possesses a flattened, centipede-like body the size of a large dog, featuring bright green skin, webbed amphibian legs, and three yellow eyes on each side of its head. Its anatomy includes jaws resembling a mix of spider mandibles and fish teeth, ending in a long stinger with a red tip. As a being capable of creating any living creature, Othaghor produces [[Elderspawn]] and other spawn that inhabit the [[island]]. The resulting hordes are feared for their variety of poisons and claws that can pierce minds and rend iron.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] is the size of a large dog and flattened like a centipede with webbed amphibian legs.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] has bright green skin and three yellow eyes on either side of its head.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] has a long red-tipped stinger at the end of its body.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]]'s jaws look like a cross between fish teeth and spider mandibles.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] has spawn that inhabit the [[island]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c10">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]]'s hordes are known for their variety of poisons.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] is a being capable of making any living creature.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c12">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] is a creator of [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c16">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The hordes of [[Othaghor]] possess claws that can rend iron and pierce minds.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] is an unknown entity.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Othaghor]] is endless biology involving breeding, consumption, and mutation.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Othaghor]] is a [[Hordefather]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c7">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Othaghor]] is something the [[Regents]] might let out for a fistfight.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c13">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“He carries with him a Bellowing Horror, a spawn of Othaghor that often declares death.”<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|And a voice like Othaghor himself bellowed out, “BEHIND YOU!”<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c26" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|“I represent the will of Othaghor, the Hordefather,” he said, his voice sounding as though his throat had been wracked by the same disease as his face.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c7" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire concepts]]
